name: How to Handle 'No Work Available' When You Have Restrictions
description: How to Handle 'No Work Available' When You Have Restrictions - Boise Idaho

step.name: Step 1
step.text: If your doctor has issued medical restrictions that prevent you from doing your normal job, follow these steps to ensure you are protected and continue to receive your benefits. This process is critical for workers in Utah, Idaho, and beyond.

step.name: Step 3
step.text: Never rely on a verbal 'the doctor said I can't lift.' You must receive a formal 'Work Status Report' or 'Restriction Note' from your clinic. Ensure it clearly lists specific physical limitations (e.g., 'no standing more than 30 minutes,' 'no overhead work').

step.name: Step 5
step.text: Hand a copy of the restrictions to your HR department or supervisor. We recommend doing this via email or getting a signed 'received' copy. Ask the specific question: 'Do you have work available that fits within these specific medical restrictions?'

step.name: Step 8
step.text: 1. **They provide Light Duty:** If they have a job that fits your restrictions, you must take it. If it pays less than your old job, you should receive 'Temporary Partial Disability' to make up 66.6% of the difference.

step.name: Step 9
step.text: 2. **They have No Work:** If they say they cannot accommodate you, they must notify the insurance company. You are then entitled to 'Temporary Total Disability' (TTD) payments.

step.name: Step 11
step.text: Once the employer confirms they have no work, your TTD checks should start within 7-14 days. If the insurance company delays or denies these payments, you must take immediate action. This is the point where most workers need a lawyer to file a 'Notice of Claim' or a 'Motion for Benefits.'

step.name: Step 13
step.text: While you are off work, you must attend every doctor's appointment. Missing one appointment gives the insurance company an excuse to say you are 'non-compliant' and stop your checks. Continue to update your employer every few weeks on your status to prove you are not 'abandoning' your job.

step.name: Step 15
step.text: Eventually, your doctor will say you have reached Maximum Medical Improvement (MMI). This means you are as healed as you are going to get. If you still have restrictions at this point, they are considered 'Permanent.' You will now move toward a permanent disability rating and a final settlement. Don't sign anything at this stage without a legal review, as this is the most important financial moment of your case.
